content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

(fun _ => sorry)`) of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` + L817 `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` Main.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
12	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced
13	
14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68	
69	## References
70	
71	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
72	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
73	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
74	- Project infrastructure consumed:
75	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
76	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
77	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
78	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
79	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
80	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
81	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
82	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
83	-/

85	set_option autoImplicit false
86	
87	universe u
88	
89	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
90	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
91	
92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
93	
94	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
95	
96	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
97	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
98	`ModuleCat k`).
99	
100	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
101	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
102	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
103	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
104	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
105	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
106	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
107	iter-132 companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (at
108	line 244 below) pins the rank to `n` from the
109	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
110	
111	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
112	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
113	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
114	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
115	
116	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
117	change).** The body is built as follows:
118	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
119	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
120	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
121	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
122	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
123	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
124	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
125	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
126	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
127	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
128	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
129	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
130	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
131	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
132	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
133	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
134	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
135	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
136	   `k`-module.
137	
138	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
139	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
140	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
141	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
142	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
143	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
144	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
145	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
146	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
147	the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
148	below) rewrites against. For the live consumer
149	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
150	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
151	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
152	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
153	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
154	
155	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The rank (`= n`) is
156	now pinned by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below;
157	closed iter-132). The free/finite structural properties
158	(`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`) are content for follow-up companion
159	lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
160	by those lemmas is witnessed here by
161	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below). -/
162	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
163	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
164	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
165	    ModuleCat k :=
166	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
167	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
168	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
169	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
170	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
171	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
172	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
173	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
174	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
175	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
176	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
177	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
178	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
179	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
180	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
181	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
182	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
183	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
184	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
185	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
186	    exact hxV
187	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
188	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
189	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
190	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
191	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
192	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
193	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
194	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
195	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
196	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
197	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
198	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
199	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
200	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
201	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
202	
203	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
204	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
205	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
206	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
207	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
208	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
209	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
210	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
211	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
212	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
213	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
214	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
215	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
216	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
217	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
218	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
219	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
220	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
221	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
222	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
223	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
224	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
225	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
226	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
227	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
228	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
229	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
230	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
231	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
232	  exact hxV
233	
234	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
235	
236	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
237	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
238	`finrank` equal to `n`.
239	
240	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
241	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
242	
243	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
244	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
245	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
246	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
247	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
248	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
249	
250	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
251	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
252	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
253	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
254	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
255	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
256	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
257	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
258	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
259	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
260	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
261	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
262	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
263	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
264	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
265	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
266	  let V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose
267	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
268	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
269	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
270	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
271	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
272	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
273	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
274	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
275	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
276	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
277	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
278	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
279	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
280	    exact hxV
281	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
282	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
283	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
284	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
285	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
286	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
287	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
288	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
289	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
290	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
291	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
292	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
293	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
294	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
295	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
296	
297	/-! ## Piece (i.b) — shear-iso globalisation of the cotangent
298	
299	The next three declarations support the iter-134+ piece (i.b) lane (see
300	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b)). Their goal is the
301	main lemma `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`:
302	
303	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom  ≅
304	      pullback_along_str_map (pullback_along_η_G (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`
305	
306	stated at the **presheaf-of-modules level** (sheaf-level RHS per the iter-133
307	mathlib-analogist Decision 4 + iter-134 plan-agent pre-commitment). The proof
308	chain has three structural pieces:
309	
310	* Step 1 (`shearMulRight`): build the binary-product shear iso
311	  `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only.
312	  This is a categorical statement (no scheme-side content); modelled on
313	  `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (one-input version,
314	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`).
315	
316	* Step 2 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`):
317	  the load-bearing presheaf-of-modules base-change identity
318	  `Ω_{(G ⊗ G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` on `(G ⊗ G).left`.
319	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (~150–300 LOC); chains
320	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv` (algebra side) with
321	  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` / `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` (presheaf side).
322	
323	* Step 3 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`):
324	  the section-restriction identity bridging `s^* pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` to
325	  `π_G^* η_G^* Ω_{G/k}` via `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` and the
326	  categorical identity `pr_2 ∘ s = η_G ∘ π_G` for `s = ⟨𝟙_G, η_G⟩`.
327	-/
328	
329	section ShearIso
330	
331	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
332	
333	/-- **Binary-product shear isomorphism** for a group object `G` in a Cartesian
334	monoidal category: the map
335	`σ = lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G`, informally `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b)`,
336	is an isomorphism. The inverse is
337	`σ⁻¹ = lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`, informally
338	`(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b)`.
339	
340	This is the binary-product upgrade of
341	`CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`),
342	which packages only the one-input version `(· · f)` for a fixed global element
343	`f : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. Here both factors of `G ⊗ G` are free.
344	
345	Piece (i.b) Step 1 (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 1 of
346	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`). NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (no packaged shear
347	iso for binary products in Mathlib; the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict
348	Decision 1 prescribes this exact construction). -/
349	@[simps]
350	def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
351	  hom := lift (fst G G) μ
352	  inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)
353	  hom_inv_id := by
354	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · (a · b)) = (a, b)`.
355	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
356	    · simp
357	    · -- Goal: `(σ.hom ≫ σ.inv) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
358	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
359	      -- Goal: `σ.hom ≫ (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) = snd G G`
360	      rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
361	      -- Goal: `lift (σ.hom ≫ (fst G G ≫ ι)) (σ.hom ≫ snd G G) ≫ μ = snd G G`
362	      rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
363	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
364	      -- Trick: use `lift_lift_assoc` with f := fst ≫ ι, g := fst, h := snd, then
365	      -- close by `lift_comp_inv_left` and `lift_comp_one_left`.
366	      rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
367	          lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
368	            rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
369	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
370	  inv_hom_id := by
371	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b) ↦ (a, a · (a⁻¹ · b)) = (a, b)`.
372	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
373	    · simp
374	    · -- Goal: `(σ.inv ≫ σ.hom) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
375	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
376	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
377	      -- Apply `lift_lift_assoc` (← direction): the RHS form `lift f (lift g h ≫ μ) ≫ μ`
378	      -- rewrites to `lift (lift f g ≫ μ) h ≫ μ` with f := fst, g := fst ≫ ι, h := snd.
379	      rw [show (lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ :
380	          G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
381	          lift (lift (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from
382	          (MonObj.lift_lift_assoc (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G)).symm,
383	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right,
384	        MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
385	
386	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
387	lemma shearMulRight_hom_fst (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
388	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G := by
389	  simp [shearMulRight]
390	
391	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
392	lemma shearMulRight_hom_snd (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
393	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ snd G G = μ := by
394	  simp [shearMulRight]
395	
396	end ShearIso
397	
398	/-! ### Helpers / main lemma for piece (i.b)
399	
400	The remaining declarations of this section state the helper sub-lemmas
401	and main lemma of piece (i.b) at the presheaf-of-modules level. Their
402	bodies are work-in-progress for iter-134+; the proofs decompose
403	along the closure path documented in
404	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b) +
405	`analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`.

407	
408	/-- **Compatibility morphism of structure presheaves of rings** along a scheme
409	morphism `f`. This is the `φ' = ((adj).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` shape used by
410	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`; we package it once here so the
411	`PresheafOfModules.pullback`-based statements below can refer to it
412	uniformly.
413	
414	Specifically, for a scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ Z`, this is the adjunction
415	transpose of the structure-sheaf morphism `f.c : O_Z ⟶ f.base _* O_Y`,
416	delivering a morphism `f.base⁻¹ O_Z ⟶ O_Y` of presheaves of rings on
417	`Y`.
418	
419	**Note**: this is **not** the φ for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, which
420	expects a morphism of presheaves on the *codomain* `Y`
421	(`Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ (Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf`) and is
422	obtained via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom`. The two conventions
423	serve distinct downstream consumers. -/
424	noncomputable def schemeHomRingCompatibility {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
425	    (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback (C := CommRingCat) f.base).obj Z.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
426	  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c

428	/-! ### Helper sub-lemmas and main lemma of piece (i.b)
429	
430	The three declarations below state the intended sheaf-level RHS
431	signatures for piece (i.b)'s closure chain (Step 2 base-change of
432	differentials, Step 3 section restriction, Compose main lemma).
433	Status: Step 3 closed iter-136 (no sorry); Step 2 PARTIAL iter-137 (body
434	remains `sorry`; inverse-direction-via-adjunction skeleton recorded in

443	
444	The compatibility morphisms for `PresheafOfModules.pullback` are
445	obtained inline as `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom <morphism>).hom`,

451	-/
452	
453	/-- The categorical identity at the underlying scheme level:
454	`s.left ≫ pr_2.left = G.hom ≫ η[G].left`, where

3	
4	This chapter is a pointer to the Lean file
5	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean}. The mathematical content
6	itself lives in chapter~\ref{chap:RigidityKbar} (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}
7	\S "Piece (i)") and is not duplicated here. This chapter exists to satisfy
8	the per-Lean-file blueprint convention (one chapter per Lean file).
9	
10	\section{Lean declarations in this file}
11	
12	\begin{itemize}
13	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
14	        --- the definition of the cotangent space at the identity as a
15	        \texttt{ModuleCat k}. Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
16	        (piece (i.a); closed iter-128, body refactored through iter-131).
17	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars}
18	        --- structural-shape acceptance lemma (piece (i.a); closed
19	        iter-131; supports both \texttt{change}-based and
20	        \texttt{obtain}+\texttt{rw} rewrite patterns downstream).
21	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}
22	        --- rank lemma (piece (i.a); closed iter-132).
23	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.
24	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight} --- the binary-product
25	        shear isomorphism $\sigma = \langle \pr_1, \mu \rangle$ in
26	        $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ (piece (i.b) Step 1; iter-134
27	        \texttt{NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL}, $\sim 30$--$60$ LOC; \emph{closed}
28	        iter-134). Built from \texttt{GrpObj} data only; modelled on
29	        \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight}. Companion \texttt{@[simps]}
30	        lemmas \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_fst} and
31	        \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_snd} expose the first/second-coordinate
32	        identifications $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_1 = \pr_1$ and
33	        $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_2 = \mu$.
34	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.schemeHomRingCompatibility} ---
35	        packaging helper around $((\mathrm{adj}).\mathrm{homEquiv}\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{symm}\,f.c$,
36	        the adjunction-transpose compatibility morphism of structure presheaves
37	        of rings along a scheme morphism $f \colon Y \to Z$. Used by
38	        \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}. Note: this is \emph{not} the
39	        $\varphi$ for \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}, which expects a
40	        morphism of presheaves on the codomain $Y$, obtained via Mathlib's
41	        \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom} (iter-135 mathlib-analogist
42	        verdict; see \texttt{analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md}).
43	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two}
44	        --- presheaf-of-modules base-change helper (piece (i.b) Step 2;
45	        iter-134+ NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC).
46	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}. Iter-138 prover
47	        landed PARTIAL with substantive Route (b) skeleton; three concrete
48	        sub-sorries remain inside this declaration's body and inside the
49	        two helpers below (iter-140 prover targets).
50	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_derivation}
51	        --- pointwise inverse-direction derivation $D$ on the transparent
52	        pushforward of $\Omega_{(G\times_k G)/G}$ along the second-projection
53	        compatibility morphism, defined via
54	        \texttt{PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk} (piece (i.b) Step 2,
55	        iter-138 helper). Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv_derivation}.
56	        The additive and Leibniz laws closed iter-138; the $d_{\app}$
57	        (zero on $\varphi_G$-image) and $d_{\map}$ (cross-open naturality)
58	        laws remain \texttt{sorry}-bodied (iter-140 prover targets).
59	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv}
60	        --- inverse morphism
61	        $(\PresheafOfModules.pullback\,\psi).\obj\,\Omega_{G/k} \to \Omega_{(G\times_k G)/G}$
62	        for the base-change iso, obtained by transposing the universal-
63	        property-derived morphism through the
64	        \texttt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction} (piece (i.b) Step 2,
65	        iter-138 helper). Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv}.
66	        Sorry-free in Lean iter-138; \texttt{IsIso} of this morphism is the
67	        third concrete iter-140 sub-sorry (Route (b'2) per iter-139
68	        analogist verdict in
69	        \texttt{analogies/isiso-basechange-along-proj-two-inv.md}).
70	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}
71	        --- section-restriction helper (piece (i.b) Step 3; iter-134+,
72	        $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC).
73	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}.
74	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}
75	        --- main piece (i.b) trivialisation lemma; presheaf-of-modules
76	        level statement; sheaf-level RHS per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist
77	        Decision 4 and iter-134 plan-agent pre-commitment.
78	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}.
79	\end{itemize}

612	  %
613	  % NOTE iter-138 (d\_app closure recipe). Let
614	  % $\varphi_G : (G.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{base}^{-1}\,\mathcal O_{\Spec k}) \to G.\mathrm{left}.\texttt{presheaf}$
615	  % be the compatibility morphism for the structure map
616	  % $G.\mathrm{hom} : G.\mathrm{left} \to \Spec k$ (the iter-135
617	  % \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom} idiom, in homEquiv-transpose
618	  % shape for the source-side of the pullback adjunction), and
619	  % $\psi : G.\mathrm{left}.\texttt{presheaf} \to (G \otimes G).\mathrm{left}.\texttt{presheaf} \circ \mathrm{snd}^{-1}$
620	  % the compatibility morphism for the second projection
621	  % $\pr_2 = \texttt{snd}\,G\,G : G \otimes G \to G$. The
622	  % \texttt{d\_app} law of the pointwise derivation requires
623	  % showing, for each open $X \in G.\mathrm{left}.\Opens^{\mathrm{op}}$
624	  % and each section $a \in (G.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{base}^{-1}\,\mathcal O_{\Spec k}).\mathrm{obj}\,X$,
625	  % the vanishing
626	  % $\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D}\,\_\,((\psi.\app\,X).\hom\,(\varphi_G.\app\,X\,a)) = 0$.
627	  %
628	  % This vanishing follows from the \textbf{categorical commutativity in
629	  % $\Over\,(\Spec k)$}: the first and second projections
630	  % $\pr_1 = \texttt{fst}\,G\,G$, $\pr_2 = \texttt{snd}\,G\,G$ of
631	  % $G \otimes G$ satisfy
632	  % $\pr_1.\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom} = \pr_2.\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom}$
633	  % as morphisms $G \otimes G \to \Spec k$ (both compositions equal
634	  % the structure map of $G \otimes G$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ — a
635	  % direct consequence of the binary-product universal property in
636	  % $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, which forces both projection morphisms to be
637	  % morphisms over $\Spec k$ and hence to commute with the structure
638	  % maps). Translating this scheme-level identity to the presheaf
639	  % level via Mathlib's \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}
640	  % functorial: the composite $(\psi.\app\,X) \circ (\varphi_G.\app\,X)$
641	  % factors through the source-presheaf morphism
642	  % $\varphi_{\texttt{LHS}}.\app\,(\mathrm{snd}^{-1}\,X)$, where
643	  % $\varphi_{\texttt{LHS}}$ is the compatibility morphism for the
644	  % structure map of $(G \otimes G)$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_1$ (the
645	  % \texttt{LHS} of the displayed iso, i.e.\
646	  % $\texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,(\texttt{fst}\,G\,G).\mathrm{left}$).
647	  % By construction of the pointwise derivation (Mathlib's
648	  % \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D}), the universal Kähler differential
649	  % vanishes on the image of the source-presheaf morphism — this is
650	  % the algebra-side \texttt{d\_app} law of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D}
651	  % packaged via \texttt{ModuleCat.Derivation.d\_app} at
652	  % \texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Basic.lean}.

654	  % \textbf{Concretely}, the Lean closure should: (1) chase the
655	  % scheme-level equality
656	  % $\pr_1.\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom} = \pr_2.\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom}$
657	  % using the $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ structural identities
658	  % (\texttt{Over.comp\_left}, \texttt{Over.toUnit\_left}, and the
659	  % universal property of the binary product $G \otimes G$ packaged
660	  % by \texttt{CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom\_ext} on the
661	  % $\texttt{toUnit}\colon G \otimes G \to \mathbf 1 = \Spec k$
662	  % morphism); (2) translate via

668	  % \texttt{ModuleCat.Derivation.d\_app} (or the underlying
669	  % \texttt{Derivation.d\_app} on
670	  % \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D}) to discharge the vanishing.

811	  % \emph{The \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} chart-opacity blocker.}
812	  % Mathlib defines \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback $\varphi$} as
813	  % $(\texttt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\varphi).\texttt{leftAdjoint}$
814	  % (a left adjoint, packaged as the abstract left adjoint of the

1075	  The construction chains two Mathlib idioms. On each affine chart $W = \Spec B$ of the source $G \times_{k} G$, the sections of both sides are Kähler-differential modules with appropriate base rings. The left-hand side $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ over $W$ gives $\Omega_{B/B_{1}}$, where $B_{1}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{1}$ to $W$. The right-hand side $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ over $W$ gives $B \otimes_{B_{2}} \Omega_{B_{2}/k}$, where $B_{2}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{2}$. The binary-product universal property in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ supplies an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}\,$k$\,$B_{1}$\,$B_{2}$\,$B$ square (the two projections $B_{1}, B_{2} \to B$ exhibit $B$ as the tensor product $B_{1} \otimes_{k} B_{2}$ at chart level). The algebra-side base-change identity
1076	  \[
1077	    \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} \colon \;\; B \,\otimes_{B_{2}}\, \Omega_{B_{2}/k} \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \Omega_{B/B_{1}}
1078	  \]
1079	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}; for an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout R S A B} square, yields $B \otimes_{A} \Omega[A/R] \simeq_{B} \Omega[B/S]$) gives the chart-level value identity. Promoting this chart-by-chart equivalence to a natural iso of presheaves of modules uses the presheaf-side functor \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) composed with the project-side chart-level value identity \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60}); naturality follows from naturality of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} and of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback}.
1080	
1081	  \emph{Mathlib name summary (NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC).} Algebra-side: \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}). Presheaf-side: \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) and \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}). Project-side: \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60--66}). This is the load-bearing piece of (i.b); without it, the shear iso of Step 1 of \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}'s proof cannot be turned into a cotangent-level iso. Mathlib has no scheme-level relative cotangent sheaf at all (per \texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 2), so this lemma is a genuine NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL contribution candidate. Compatible upstream Mathlib PR-shape: a sheaf-of-modules version of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} promoting \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-of-charts to a presheaf-level natural iso.
1082	\end{proof}

1320	\[
1321	  \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars\,$\psi_V$.hom).obj\,(ModuleCat.of\,$\Gamma$(G, V)\,$\Omega$[$\Gamma$(G, V)\,/\,$\Gamma$(Spec k, U)])},
1322	\]
1323	with no top-level \texttt{Classical.choice} head symbol. 1325	Two downstream rewrite patterns are available, both mathematically equivalent (the body's underlying \texttt{TensorProduct} carrier and the \texttt{extendScalars}-of-\texttt{ModuleCat.of} form share the same \texttt{Classical.choose} extraction, hence are definitionally equal once the \texttt{ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'} carrier identity is unfolded):
1326	
1327	\begin{itemize}
1328	  \item \textbf{Direct \texttt{change}-based route (primary; used by the iter-132 rank-lemma closure).} The iter-132 close of \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} (in-tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}) uses a direct \texttt{change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct $\Gamma$(G, V) k $\Omega$[\ldots]) = n} step that exposes the body's underlying \texttt{TensorProduct} carrier via the definitional unfolding of \texttt{ModuleCat.extendScalars} (whose \texttt{obj'} is \texttt{TensorProduct $\Gamma$(G, V) k M} up to \texttt{ModuleCat} coercion, per Mathlib's \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings:396}). 1339	
1340	The named declaration $\thm{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}$ is consumed by:
1341	
1342	\begin{itemize}
1343	  \item \textbf{M2.b genus-0 witness} (iter-127 scaffold $\thm{def:genusZeroWitness}$, then later body iter-145+). The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field of \texttt{JacobianWitness C} for a genus-$0$ curve $C$ over $k$ branches on whether $C(k) \neq \emptyset$. When $C(k) \neq \emptyset$, \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} is invoked \textbf{directly over $k$} (post-iter-127 over-k commitment; no base-change-and-descent step); the marked point $P \in C(k)$ supplied by the universal quantifier feeds into the rigidity declaration's pointing hypothesis. When $C(k) = \emptyset$ (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the field is \textbf{vacuously true} at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\ldots$ is trivially satisfied when the type $\mathbf{1} \to C$ is empty.
1344	  \item \textbf{No other in-tree consumer.} The declaration is project-internal; it is not consumed by any \texttt{Cohomology/*.lean}, \texttt{Genus.lean}, \texttt{AbelJacobi.lean}, or \texttt{Differentials.lean} declaration.
1345	\end{itemize}

39	- `task_results/Cotangent_GrpObj.lean.md` — iter-140 prover task result with §"d_app: detailed gap" L68–L108 and §"d_map: detailed gap" L111–L143.
40	
41	## Mathlib infrastructure verified this iter
42	
43	All names below were verified via `lean_local_search` + `lean_loogle` +
44	`lean_run_code` typecheck against the project's pinned Mathlib.
45	
46	| Name | Module:Line | Used by |
47	|---|---|---|
48	| `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_map` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic:80` | d_app (the streamlined factoring-lemma closure) |
49	| `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` (`d_map` field default `:= by simp`) | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic:47–62` | d_app underlying field |
50	| `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic:106` | both |
51	| `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward:86` | d_map |
52	| `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward:95–97` | d_map (the missing-named explicit unfolding lemma) |
53	| `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply'` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward:99–106` | d_map (`@[simp]`-normal form; preferred for `simp only`) |
54	| `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'_map_d` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf:201–207` | d_map |
55	| `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d` | `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic:152–155` | d_map (underlying ring-map base-change derivation commutation) |
56	| `NatTrans.naturality` (for ψ) | applied via `Scheme.Hom.c.naturality` | d_map (ψ-naturality piece) |
57	
58	## The critical missing-name observation (d_map)
59	
60	The blueprint's claim of "definitional/transparent per
61	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:39, 86`"
62	was technically correct at the *underlying LinearMap-application level*:
63	the unfolding `(((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.op.map f) m`
64	is `rfl` (verified standalone via `lean_run_code` this iter; both the
65	named `pushforward_obj_map_apply` and direct `rfl` close it).
66	
67	**But** the iter-140 prover's `change`-based approach failed because:
68	
69	1. The `pushforward₀_obj` and `pushforward₀` definitions in Mathlib
70	   (`Pushforward.lean:37, 55`) are explicitly annotated with
71	   `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`. This
72	   instructs Lean's `isDefEq` (and hence `whnf`) NOT to aggressively
73	   unfold these definitions during definitional-equality checks.
74	
75	2. `change` invokes the kernel `whnf`-based `isDefEq` to align the goal
76	   with the target shape. When the d_map goal is wrapped inside the
77	   `Derivation'.mk` lambda context with floating metavariables (`?m.161,
78	   .162, .130, .91` per iter-140 task result), `whnf` has to traverse a
79	   deeply-nested adjunction-transposed expression including
80	   `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv` + `restrictScalars` + the
81	   `pushforward₀_obj` opacity wall. With `respectTransparency false` on
82	   `pushforward₀`, `whnf` cannot punch through this wall, and runs out
83	   of heartbeats at 200000.
84	
85	3. **The right idiom**: bypass `whnf` entirely. Use the named lemma
86	   `pushforward_obj_map_apply'` (`@[simp]`-normal form) via `simp only
87	   [pushforward_obj_map_apply']` (which applies a single rewrite without
88	   `whnf` traversal). This delivers the unfolding the blueprint promised
89	   without paying the `whnf` cost. Then `change` (or further `simp only`)
90	   succeeds against the now-unfolded goal.
91	
92	In iter-138 language: this is **not** a structural side-step (like the
93	helper-pair refactor for iter-138's `pullback` chart-opacity); it is a
94	**`NEEDS_MATHLIB_LEMMA_NAME`** verdict. The Mathlib lemma exists; the
95	blueprint correctly pointed to the file but did not name the lemma; the
96	iter-140 prover did not search for the named lemma and relied on
97	transparency that the `respectTransparency false` annotation explicitly
98	disables.
99	
100	## The streamlined d_app pattern
101	
102	The iter-140 prover's standalone-validated factoring-lemma recipe is
103	*correct in shape* but does extra work that the Mathlib API already
104	packages. The iter-140 version:
105	
106	```lean
107	example (A B C : CommRingCat) (f1 : A ⟶ B) (g : C ⟶ B) (k : A ⟶ C)
108	    (hcomm : k ≫ g = f1) (a : A) :
109	    (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g).d (f1.hom a) = 0 := by
110	  have heq : f1.hom a = g.hom (k.hom a) := by rw [← hcomm]; rfl
111	  rw [heq]
112	  letI : Algebra C B := g.hom.toAlgebra
113	  letI : Module C (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential g) :=
114	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap C B)
115	  letI : IsScalarTower C B (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential g) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
117	  exact (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g :
118	    Derivation C B (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential g)).map_algebraMap _
119	```
120	
121	The streamlined version (verified this iter via `lean_run_code`):
122	
123	```lean
124	example (A B C : CommRingCat) (f1 : A ⟶ B) (g : C ⟶ B) (k : A ⟶ C)
125	    (hcomm : k ≫ g = f1) (a : A) :
126	    (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g).d (f1.hom a) = 0 := by
127	  rw [show f1.hom a = g.hom (k.hom a) from by rw [← hcomm]; rfl]
128	  exact (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g).d_map _
129	```
130	
131	This uses `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_map` (Basic.lean:80) which IS exactly
132	the `map_algebraMap`-based zero-on-image lemma the iter-140 version
133	hand-rolls. Mathlib already has the `Algebra` / `Module` /
134	`IsScalarTower` instance discharge bundled inside `ModuleCat.Derivation`.
135	
136	LOC saving: 4 LOC per use; clearer intent.
137	
138	## Decisions identified
139	
140	### Decision 1: d_app closure recipe shape
141	
142	- **Mathlib idiom**: `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_map`
143	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic:80`) — the
144	  `@[simp]` lemma `D.d (f a) = 0` for derivations over a ring map.
145	  Plus the categorical-witness construction of `h : Source ⟶ Target`
146	  factoring `(ψ.app X) ∘ (φ_G.app X)` through `φ_LHS.app (snd⁻¹X)`,
147	  which has no Mathlib shortcut (it's the project's bespoke
148	  categorical-chase work).
149	- **Project's current path**: iter-140 prover's standalone-validated
150	  factoring-lemma recipe — sound in shape, with the streamlining
151	  recommendation above (`d_map` for `letI ... + map_algebraMap`).
152	- **Gap**: identical (recipe shape) modulo the 4-LOC streamlining.
153	- **Cost of divergence (if any)**: ~4 LOC of redundant instance
154	  discharge per call site; minor.
155	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with iter-140 shape; recommend `d_map`
156	  streamlining at writing time.
157	
158	### Decision 2: Construction of the factoring witness `h` (d_app)
159	
160	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE PACKAGED. The factoring witness for d_app
161	  comes from chasing `(fst G G).w + (snd G G).w` (the `Over (Spec k)`
162	  morphism property), via `LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c_app`, transposed
163	  through `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv.symm`. Each ingredient
164	  is a Mathlib lemma; there is no one-shot combiner.
165	- **Project's current path**: per blueprint `RigidityKbar.tex:643–659`
166	  + iter-140 prover recipe step 4 — construct `h` from the categorical
167	  identity in 3 stages (categorical equality in `Over (Spec k)`,
168	  scheme-level lift via `comp_c_app`, adjunction-transpose). Each stage
169	  is a `simp`/`erw`-driven step.
170	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — the categorical chase is bespoke;
171	  no equivalent is packaged.
172	- **Cost of divergence**: ~40–80 LOC of categorical chase (iter-140
173	  prover estimate confirmed).
174	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**; the project must build it.
175	  The closure recipe shape (factoring lemma + `d_map`) is the
176	  Mathlib-canonical algebra-side step; the witness construction is
177	  project-specific.
178	
179	### Decision 3: d_map closure — the missing-name explicit unfolding lemma
180	
181	- **Mathlib idiom**: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply'`
182	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward:99–106`,
183	  `@[simp]`-normal form). This is the `simp`-canonical underlying
184	  LinearMap-application identity
185	  `(((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m`,
186	  proved by `rfl` (verified this iter standalone via `lean_run_code`).
187	- **Project's current path**: iter-140 prover attempted `change
188	  (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D _).d _ = _` — which invokes `whnf`
189	  through the `pushforward₀` opacity wall (where
190	  `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` is explicitly
191	  set at `Pushforward.lean:37, 55`). This caused a deterministic
192	  heartbeat timeout at 200000.
193	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong — the prover's approach was
194	  fundamentally incompatible with Mathlib's transparency annotation;
195	  the blueprint correctly identified the file but did not name the
196	  lemma.
197	- **Cost of divergence**: would block all d_map closure attempts via
198	  `change`-based approaches at any heartbeat budget; the
199	  `respectTransparency false` is a guard, not a performance issue.
200	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_LEMMA_NAME** — the lemma is
201	  `pushforward_obj_map_apply'` (or the underlying `pushforward_obj_map_apply`
202	  for explicit `rw`). Re-route the recipe through this lemma.
203	
204	### Decision 4: d_map closure recipe shape (after Decision 3 fix)
205	
206	- **Mathlib idiom**: three-step chase using:
207	  1. `pushforward_obj_map_apply'` (RHS unfolding via `simp only`).
208	  2. `NatTrans.naturality` (or `NatTrans.naturality_apply`) for ψ =
209	     `(toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom` (which is `(snd G G).left.c`
210	     whiskered with `forget₂` per `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf:42–45`).
211	  3. `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'_map_d`
212	     (Presheaf.lean:201–207, `@[simp]`-tagged) — commutation of the
213	     universal Kähler derivation with `R.map f`.
214	- **Project's current path** (after fix): inside the `Derivation'.mk`
215	  d_map field lambda
216	  `fun X Y f x => by ... sorry`, the closure body:
217	  ```lean
218	  fun X Y f x => by
219	    -- Step 1: unfold the pushforward .map via the named lemma
220	    simp only [pushforward_obj_map_apply']
221	    -- Step 2: rewrite via ψ-naturality
222	    have hψ := NatTrans.naturality_apply
223	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom f x
224	    -- Step 3: use the four-step "have h := ... ; change ... ; rw [h] ; exact ..."
225	    -- pattern (from iter-138 codified d_add/d_mul closures) to chain
226	    -- ψ-naturality with relativeDifferentials'_map_d.
227	    -- (Details depend on the exact metavariable shape at the call site.)
228	    sorry
229	  ```
230	- **Gap**: identical to Mathlib idiom once Decision 3's named lemma is
231	  threaded in.
232	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-route via the named
233	  unfolding lemma + ψ-naturality + `relativeDifferentials'_map_d`.
234	
235	### Decision 5: LOC envelope for d_app + d_map combined
236	
237	- **d_app** (Decision 1 + Decision 2 combined): ~50–90 LOC.
238	  - ~5–10 LOC: the algebra-side discharge using the streamlined
239	    `d_map` lemma.
240	  - ~40–80 LOC: the categorical-witness construction of `h` (the

245	  - ~10–15 LOC: ψ-naturality `have` + `change` / `rw` chain (mimicking
246	    the iter-138 d_add/d_mul four-step pattern).
247	  - ~10–15 LOC: `relativeDifferentials'_map_d` discharge with
248	    metavariable cleanup.
249	  - ~5–15 LOC: type-noise smoothing (`erw`, `dsimp`, restrictScalars
250	    fiddling).
251	- **Combined**: ~80–140 LOC.
252	- **Envelope check**: cumulative (i.b)-side build entering iter-141 is
253	  ~485 LOC (iter-134 + iter-136 + iter-138 + iter-140 deltas per
254	  directive). Adding ~80–140 LOC for d_app + d_map yields ~565–625 LOC
255	  cumulative, well within the 1000 LOC envelope from iter-137
256	  renormalised piece (i.b). **Comfortably fits.**
257

47	       (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g).d (f1.hom a) = 0 := by
48	     have heq : f1.hom a = g.hom (k.hom a) := by rw [← hcomm]; rfl
49	     rw [heq]
50	     letI : Algebra C B := g.hom.toAlgebra
51	     letI : Module C (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential g) :=
52	       Module.compHom _ (algebraMap C B)
53	     letI : IsScalarTower C B (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential g) :=
54	       IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
55	     exact (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D g :

94	   such that
95	   `h ≫ (φ_LHS.app (snd⁻¹X)) = (φ_G.app X) ≫ (ψ.app X)`.
